active_projects.description: Fix task number parsing in wezterm-task-number.sh to support multi-task syntax (/research 7, 22-24), additional commands (/spawn N, /task --recover N, /task --expand N), and prevent stale task numbers. Ensure tab always shows {N} {root} format with #{task} when applicable.

artifacts.summary: Complete command inventory (6 missing patterns), 3-tier parsing logic (SET/CLEAR/PRESERVE), multi-task compact display strategy, 4 hook copies + 2 doc files to update
2-phase implementation plan for 3-tier task number parsing with multi-task spec support

memory_candidates.content: WezTerm task number hook uses 3-tier UserPromptSubmit logic: (1) Workflow command with task number -> SET TASK_NUMBER to compact spec; (2) Any slash command without task number -> CLEAR TASK_NUMBER; (3) Free text / follow-up answer -> PRESERVE (no change). The preserve tier prevents stale-on-follow-up where answering an agent question during a workflow would clear the task number display.

Multi-task syntax in wezterm-task-number.sh: capture the full task spec with [0-9][0-9, -]* then post-process: strip from first -- (removes flags like --team), trim trailing spaces/commas. Display as compact spec with spaces removed (e.g. 7, 22-24, 59 -> 7,22-24,59).

active_projects.description: Expand wezterm tab color palette with per-artifact-type colors (report=green, plan=blue, summary=gold, error=red, needs_input=gray). Add preflight tab coloring via UserPromptSubmit hook to show in-progress states (researching, planning, implementing). Include artifact type in signal file so wezterm can distinguish. Update wezterm.lua (nix-managed at ~/.dotfiles/config/wezterm.lua).

Refactor TTS and wezterm notification dispatch to dual-dispatch architecture: postflight fires TTS+color immediately (compatible with never-stopping workflows), signal file serves as suppress flag so Stop hook doesn't duplicate. Remove Stage 8a from skills, stub lifecycle-notify.sh. Sync to OpenCode tree.

artifacts.summary: Full audit of notification system: 3 interlocked bugs identified, signal-file + Stop hook architecture designed, 14 files inventoried, race-safe atomic consume pattern, subagent suppression via agent_id check, OpenCode divergence documented
Revised plan v2: dual-dispatch architecture (postflight immediate + Stop hook suppress-flag) compatible with never-stopping workflows

memory_candidates.content: Signal-file + Stop hook pattern for Claude Code lifecycle notifications: update-task-status.sh writes a status value to .claude/tmp/lifecycle-signal as its last postflight action. A unified claude-stop-notify.sh in the Stop hook reads and atomically consumes the file (using mv rename for race-safe single-consumer semantics). If signal present: announces lifecycle status via wezterm-notify.sh + tts-notify.sh --lifecycle. If not: falls back to needs_input + bare Tab N TTS. This replaces the unreliable Stage 8a markdown-instruction pattern where agents would call lifecycle-notify.sh.

Subagent Stop hook suppression: The Claude Code Stop hook fires for BOTH main agent and subagent turns. To suppress TTS/color updates in subagent contexts, read stdin JSON and check for agent_id field (only present in subagent Stop contexts).

OpenCode wezterm-notify.sh divergence: The .opencode/ copies of wezterm-notify.sh hardcode needs_input instead of accepting a STATUS parameter like the .claude/ version does. Similarly, opencode tts-notify.sh lacks --lifecycle mode. Any lifecycle notification feature must update both trees in parallel.

Signal-file pattern for cross-concern hook communication: when a postflight script needs to communicate state to a later-firing Stop hook, write a signal file as the LAST action (after all state updates), then have the Stop hook atomically consume it via mv-rename.

active_projects.description: Fix Neovim rendering corruption after system sleep in WezTerm. Primary root cause: yanky.nvim system_clipboard.sync_with_ring = true triggers a blocking wl-paste call via FocusGained on wake. On Wayland/GNOME, wl-paste can hang indefinitely when the compositor clipboard state is stale after sleep, freezing the entire Neovim TUI. Solution: replace yanky.nvim with a custom yank ring module (~460 LOC across 6 modules under lua/neotex/yank/) that uses vim.system() with a 2-second timeout for all clipboard reads. Includes post-sleep rendering recovery autocommands (mode, redraw!, treesitter invalidation). Research completed in dotfiles task 59 -- 3 research reports copied over.

artifacts.summary: Root cause analysis: yanky.nvim system_clipboard.sync_with_ring triggers blocking wl-paste on FocusGained after sleep. Secondary: missing VimResume/FocusGained recovery autocommands.
Feature audit of yanky.nvim usage, alternative plugin comparison, custom implementation feasibility (~200 LOC), and non-blocking clipboard sync design via vim.system().
Complete custom yank ring architecture: 6 modules under lua/neotex/yank/ (~460 LOC), production-quality code sketches, plugin spec, and file change list.
Implementation summary: 5 phases, 6 modules created, yanky.nvim replaced, all verified with nvim --headless
Diagnostic report: implementation attempt reverted. Blinking persists even with all visual operations disabled, ruling out our code. Issue is at Neovim/WezTerm/Wayland clipboard provider layer.

Team research (4 teammates): Root cause confirmed as wl-copy focus stealing on GNOME Wayland + provider caching preventing OSC 52 override. Fix: set vim.g.clipboard early in options.lua before clipboard=unnamedplus.
Refined 4-module yank ring architecture (~235 LOC): ring.lua, highlight.lua, telescope.lua, init.lua + plugin spec. No clipboard.lua or recovery.lua needed.
Refined 3-phase plan: core modules, plugin spec wiring, reference cleanup. 2 hours estimated.
Implementation summary: 3 phases, yanky.nvim replaced with custom yank ring

active_projects.completion_summary: Replaced yanky.nvim with custom 4-module yank ring: ring.lua (circular buffer), highlight.lua (vim.hl.on_yank wrapper), telescope.lua (history picker), init.lua (entry point). Removed all yanky.nvim dependencies from telescope.lua, which-key.lua, and tools/init.lua. Deleted yanky.lua.

active_projects.description: Restrict TTS announcements to lifecycle transitions (researched/planned/completed) and interactive prompts (permission_prompt/elicitation_dialog). Remove Stop hook TTS, remove idle_prompt trigger, move lifecycle TTS to fire after artifact linking, simplify tts-notify.sh by removing dead normal mode and signal file mechanism.

memory_candidates.content: TTS hook tts-notify.sh has 4 copies: .claude/hooks/ (current, 275 lines with B+A Hybrid), .claude/extensions/core/hooks/ (old, 178 lines), .opencode/hooks/ (old, 178 lines), .opencode/extensions/core/hooks/ (old, 178 lines identical to opencode/hooks). When updating tts-notify.sh, all four copies must be synchronized.

skill-nix-implementation uses a divergent postflight stage format: numbered 4-8 (not Stage N: prefix), combines status update + artifact linking in stage 5 inline jq rather than calling update-task-status.sh. All other extension skills (nvim, nix-research) follow the standard Stage 1-11 format matching core skills.

artifacts.summary: Full audit of TTS system: 3 trigger paths, 4 hook copies, 8 skills requiring Stage 8a, settings.json changes across 4 files, proposed lifecycle-notify.sh design, and complete file change table (24 files).
5-phase implementation plan to restrict TTS to lifecycle transitions and interactive prompts across 24 files
Implementation summary: 5 phases executed across settings, hooks, scripts, 12 skills, and documentation

active_projects.completion_summary: Restricted TTS announcements to lifecycle transitions (via new lifecycle-notify.sh called in skill Stage 8a) and interactive prompts (permission_prompt/elicitation_dialog); removed Stop hook TTS, removed idle_prompt, simplified tts-notify.sh, and updated all documentation.

artifacts.summary: Comprehensive analysis of Neovim and WezTerm configurations showing no automatic directory-changing behavior
Research report documenting OAuth2 token expiration as root cause and fix procedure
3-phase implementation plan for fixing Gmail OAuth2 authentication

active_projects.description: Investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills to enable CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing, following corrected understanding of fork mechanics from official Claude Code docs

artifacts.summary: Revised plan to investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills, enabling FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing with 4 phases: audit, pilot, rollout, documentation

Definitive architecture analysis: hybrid inline-agent approach has no precedent, context: fork + orchestration IS proven (slide skills), cache sharing is fundamentally incompatible with named agent routing, recommend rescoping task 500 to documentation-only

memory_candidates.content: All loaded extension skills (neovim-research, neovim-implementation, nix-research, nix-implementation) use the same Pattern A delegation as core skills -- explicit Task tool with subagent_type and multi-stage postflight. They do NOT use context: fork or agent: frontmatter. Only unloaded extension skills (e.g., slide-planning, slide-critic) use Pattern B with context: fork.

The present extension's skill-slide-planning and skill-slide-critic skills use context: fork + agent: in frontmatter AND explicit subagent_type in their Task call. This proves that context: fork with multi-stage orchestration (preflight, interactive Q&A, delegation, postflight) works correctly.
Claude Code agent definitions support a skills field that preloads skill content into the subagent context at startup. This is described as the inverse of context: fork.

active_projects.description: Optimize team-mode skills (team-research, team-plan, team-implement) to maximize FORK_SUBAGENT parallel cache sharing benefits for teammates 2-N

artifacts.summary: Team-mode skills are already eligible for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing; optimization focuses on prompt restructuring, dynamic token multiplier calculation, and cache-aware metadata reporting.
4-phase plan to restructure team-mode prompts for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing, replace hardcoded token multiplier with dynamic formula, and update documentation

memory_candidates.content: TeammateTool spawns in team-mode skills do NOT specify subagent_type, making them eligible for CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing. Teammates 2-N inherit the parent's prompt cache prefix, reducing input token cost by ~90%. Both CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 and CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 must be enabled. The actual cache benefit depends on the byte-identical shared prefix ratio -- prompts should put shared content first and teammate-specific assignments last to maximize overlap.

skill-team-research hardcodes team_size=4 in Stage 1 regardless of user-provided --team-size flag. The four fixed roles are: Primary (A), Alternatives (B), Critic (C), Horizons (D). The token_usage_multiplier is hardcoded at 5.0 in all team skill metadata but should be dynamically calculated.
